data.narrative:
Proxima Fusion is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Francesco Sciortino, Lucio Milanese, Jorrit Lion, Jonathan Schilling, and Martin Kubie that has raised €200M to date. The valuation universe for comparable analysis includes 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private nuclear energy companies, with the most relevant private comparables being Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. The nuclear energy sector contains only 1 same-stage comparable and 1 adjacent-stage comparable, falling short of the minimum 3 datapoints required for statistical reliability. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the concentration of capital, technology risk, and regulatory dynamics unique to nuclear energy. No triangulated valuation has been produced pending additional comparable data or alternative methodologies.

The primary constraint on valuation synthesis is the thinness of the nuclear energy comparable set at this stage and scale. Any forward valuation for Proxima Fusion would require either (i) expansion of the peer set through recent pricing rounds of Fuse Energy, Kairos Power, or X-Energy; (ii) application of venture-stage risk multiples and cash-burn models; or (iii) market-derived multiples from the three public nuclear names, recognizing material differences in maturity, near-term revenue visibility, and financing risk. Current data does not support a narrow valuation range.
